overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted professional social media presence across Bluesky, LinkedIn, and Mastodon, with a total following of 3,580. His content effectively caters to an academic and research-oriented audience, focusing heavily on computational social science, disinformation studies, and academic recruitment. Across all platforms, he enjoys healthy engagement, leveraging LinkedIn for major milestones and recruitment, and Bluesky/Mastodon for real-time academic discussion and software releases.

contentThemes.description: Research findings, paper publications, and event presentations surrounding the tracking of information operations, coordinated link sharing, and disinformation.
Announcements of new research grants, postdoctoral job openings, and PhD opportunities within his research team.
Updates and releases of software and algorithms designed for social science research, such as the CooRnet package.

recommendations: Increase posting frequency on Mastodon and LinkedIn to match the consistency seen on Bluesky, as both platforms show highly receptive audiences.
Include direct links, visual graphics, or code snippets when announcing tool updates (like CooRnet v2.0) to improve click-through and repost metrics.
Leverage LinkedIn's larger follower base by sharing more bite-sized summaries or key takeaways from newly published papers to drive higher scientific engagement.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky currently delivers the highest average engagement rate (6.14%), showcasing a highly active academic community that is particularly responsive to new research papers and PhD recruitment drives.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides a highly focused niche audience interested in open science and technical tool updates like CooRnet, though content volume remains low.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is the largest platform by follower size (1,850) and excels at professional milestones (grant awards) and recruitment, driving the highest absolute number of likes and comments.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
